metadata.description: Sample assessment for Arcline Health, an L2.5 Series B healthtech startup. Good tool config and commit hygiene, but significant gaps in eval gates and AI observability. Verdict: READY_FOR_PILOT, Track B.
metadata.samplePurpose: Demonstrates Track B routing for teams in the Developing band

interviewScores.name: AI Tooling Landscape
keyFindings: Claude Code deployed via Bedrock across all 3 squads
CLAUDE.md exists but is inconsistent across repos (2 of 5 repos have it)
Kiro IDE adopted by 8 of 14 developers
Standardized on Claude 3.5 Sonnet for code generation

interviewScores.name: Development Workflow & Specs
keyFindings: Feature specs used informally by senior engineers but not mandated
No bug fix or refactor spec templates in use
PRD-to-spec mapping attempted but not systematic
Team lead has drafted a spec template but it is not adopted yet

interviewScores.name: CI/CD & Quality
keyFindings: GitHub Actions with lint, test, and build steps
No eval gates -- all PRs go through human review only
Test coverage at 68%, above average for the stage
HIPAA compliance checks in CI are manual

interviewScores.name: Metrics & Visibility
keyFindings: Basic GitHub Insights used for commit activity
No AI-specific metrics (acceptance rate, cost, token usage)
Engineering manager tracks velocity in Jira but not AI contribution
No executive dashboard or readout process

interviewScores.name: Governance & Security
keyFindings: HIPAA-aware: team avoids sending PHI to AI tools
No formal AI governance policy documented
Data handling guidelines exist for prod but not for AI tools
Security team has reviewed Bedrock data residency

interviewScores.name: Org & Culture
keyFindings: VP Engineering is the executive sponsor, actively engaged
Dedicated AI champion (senior engineer) driving adoption
All 3 squads have at least one AI-enthusiastic member
Team ran an internal AI hackathon last quarter

workshopModules.name: CLAUDE.md & Standards
workshopModules.reason: Align existing config to PRISM standards

preWork.task: Install Claude Code and configure for AWS Bedrock
Run verify-setup.sh and confirm green output
Identify 2 features the team will build during the workshop
Ensure AWS account has EventBridge, Timestream, and CloudWatch access
Identify the primary CI/CD pipeline to instrument
Get approval for GitHub webhook integration
Designate an executive sponsor for the weekly readout

gapRemediation.action: Deploy the EventBridge-Timestream metrics pipeline. Enable token tracking, cost attribution, and latency monitoring.
Define quality rubrics for AI-generated code. Implement automated scoring. Establish minimum acceptance thresholds.
Identify first candidate for multi-step agent workflow. Start with low-risk automation. Document the workflow pattern.

milestones.measurable: All components live, first metrics flowing

milestones.milestone: Eval gate in CI, dashboards active
milestones.measurable: PR pipeline includes eval step, QuickSight live
AI acceptance rate 30%+, exec readout active
Policy document published to team wiki
Metrics stable, no regression below L2.5

saTouchpoints.agenda: Review pre-work checklist, confirm AWS access, workshop logistics
Deliver all 6 modules, hands-on exercises, bootstrapper deployment
Verify bootstrapper deployment, review first metrics, troubleshoot
Review dashboard data quality, flag any pipeline issues
Midpoint review, acceptance rate check, adjust pilot targets
Review progress toward 8-week goals, flag risks
Final pilot metrics, L3 readiness determination, next steps
